schema.appDescription: Estimate the right health insurance coverage for individuals and families in India. Based on age, city, and family size.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Health Insurance Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the minimum health insurance cover needed in India?
faq.a: For a family of 4 in a metro city: minimum ₹10 lakh family floater, ideally ₹15–25 lakh. Individual cover for age 30–35: ₹10 lakh. Hospitalisation costs in Mumbai private hospitals can reach ₹8–15 lakh for serious conditions like cancer, cardiac surgery, or ICU stays. Inadequate cover can wipe out savings.

faq.q: Family floater vs individual health insurance — which is better?
faq.a: Family floater: one policy covers all family members, any member can use the full sum insured. Cheaper premium than individual policies. Risk: if two members are hospitalised simultaneously, coverage may be insufficient. Individual policies are better if any member has pre-existing conditions.

faq.q: What is not covered in standard health insurance?
faq.a: Common exclusions: pre-existing diseases (waiting period 2–4 years), maternity (waiting period 9 months–4 years), dental treatment (unless accident), congenital diseases, cosmetic procedures, experimental treatments, suicide/self-injury. Always read the policy document carefully.
No. All calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: Family of 3 in Bangalore — coverage needed
examples.text: Parents (35, 32) with 1 child in Bangalore: recommended ₹20 lakh family floater. Private hospital room in Bangalore: ₹8,000–₹25,000/day. A 7-day cardiac care hospitalisation can cost ₹10–15 lakh at Apollo/Fortis.

examples.heading: Senior citizen health insurance
examples.text: Health insurance for parents aged 60–65: individual policies of ₹10 lakh each (floater risky given age). Premium for 65-year-old: ₹50,000–₹80,000/year. Consider Star Senior Citizen or Niva Bupa Senior First. Always buy before they exceed the maximum entry age (usually 65–70).
